analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet defends America's environmental record as exceptional, accuses critics of hypocrisy for ignoring China's pollution, and frames such critics as motivated by anti-American bias rather than environmental concern. Evaluation by narrative: US1 (The US has an honorable tradition) – Partially relevant but not directly invoked; the tweet praises current performance rather than historical tradition or legacy, so not a fit. US2 (admirable society) – Not addressed; no mention of society, groups, companies, or heroes. US3 (hostile enemies) – The tweet portrays unnamed critics as hostile to America and implies China as a problematic actor (rampant pollution). It frames adversarial actors undermining the US, which aligns with highlighting hostile adversaries; therefore, supported. US4 (force for good) – By asserting an "exceptional environmental record," it implies the US contributes positively to global environmental well-being; this fits portraying the US as doing good internationally, so supported. US5 (great international ally) – Not mentioned. US6 (at the forefront) – Claiming an exceptional environmental record positions the US as a leader in environmental performance; this aligns with being at the forefront, so supported. Conclusion: US3, US4, and US6 are promoted.
